History

117 solved questions in this topic · SSC GD General Knowledge & General Awareness

Home
Q.No 36 HistoryEnglish
Who mentioned the role of the Banjaras in transporting grain in his memoirs?
A
Babur
B
Humayun
C
Sher Shah Suri
D
Jahangir
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Emperor Jahangir, in his memoirs (the Tuzuk-i-Jahangiri / Jahangirnama), described the Banjaras as itinerant traders who transported grain on bullocks across the country.

A is incorrect: Babur's memoirs (Baburnama) focus on his conquests and observations of India, not the Banjaras' grain trade.

B is incorrect: Humayun did not leave such memoirs; his life was recorded by others (e.g. the Humayun-nama by Gulbadan Begum).

C is incorrect: Sher Shah Suri is known for administrative reforms and the Grand Trunk Road, not for memoirs on the Banjaras.

💡 The Banjaras were a mobile trading community who moved goods in large caravans called 'tandas'.
Q.No 37 HistoryEnglish
What term was used to describe the student stage of life in early Hindu philosophy?
A
Vanaprastha
B
Brahmachari
✓ Correct
C
Sannyasin
D
Grihastha

Solution

Correct answer: B. In the ancient Hindu ashrama system, 'Brahmacharya' was the first stage of life - the celibate student stage devoted to learning under a guru. A student was called a Brahmachari.

A is incorrect: Vanaprastha is the third stage - gradual retirement and withdrawal from worldly life.

C is incorrect: Sannyasin refers to the fourth stage - renunciation of all worldly attachments.

D is incorrect: Grihastha is the second stage - the householder/family life.

💡 The four ashramas are Brahmacharya, Grihastha, Vanaprastha and Sannyasa.
Q.No 40 HistoryEnglish
To whom did the rebel soldiers of the Meerut Regiment want to hand over the rule of the country?
A
Bahadur Shah Zafar
✓ Correct
B
Peshwa Baji Rao Il
C
Nana Saheb
D
Rani Lakshmibai

Solution

Correct answer: A. The rebel sepoys of the Meerut regiment marched to Delhi in May 1857 and proclaimed the aged Mughal emperor Bahadur Shah Zafar II as the symbolic ruler of Hindustan.

B is incorrect: Peshwa Baji Rao II had died in 1851; it was his adopted heir Nana Saheb who led the revolt at Kanpur.

C is incorrect: Nana Saheb led the rebellion in Kanpur, but the Meerut sepoys went to Delhi to the Mughal emperor.

D is incorrect: Rani Lakshmibai led the revolt at Jhansi, not the figure the Meerut soldiers turned to.

💡 After the revolt was crushed, Bahadur Shah Zafar was exiled to Rangoon, where he died in 1862.
Q.No 38 HistoryHindi
बिम्बिसार की कथित तौर पर हत्या करने या उसे आत्महत्या के लिए मजबूर करने के बाद उसका उत्तराधिकारी कौन बना था?
A
अशोक
B
बिंदुसार
C
अजातशत्रु
✓ Correct
D
चंद्रगुप्त मौर्य

Solution

Correct answer: C. According to tradition, Ajatashatru succeeded his father Bimbisara as ruler of Magadha after imprisoning and reportedly causing his death.

A is incorrect: Ashoka was a much later Mauryan emperor, not Bimbisara's successor.

B is incorrect: Bindusara was a Mauryan king (Ashoka's father), unrelated to Bimbisara's succession.

D is incorrect: Chandragupta Maurya founded the Mauryan empire long after Bimbisara's Haryanka dynasty.

💡 Bimbisara and Ajatashatru belonged to the Haryanka dynasty of Magadha and were contemporaries of the Buddha and Mahavira.
Q.No 39 HistoryEnglish
निम्नलिखित में से किस वर्ष में औरंगजेब की मृत्यु हुई थी?
A
1707
✓ Correct
B
1717
C
1747
D
1757

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Mughal emperor Aurangzeb died in 1707 (at Ahmednagar), an event that is often taken to mark the beginning of the decline of the Mughal Empire.

B is incorrect: 1717 is incorrect; by then Farrukhsiyar was on the throne.

C is incorrect: 1747 is unrelated to Aurangzeb's death.

D is incorrect: 1757 is the year of the Battle of Plassey, not Aurangzeb's death.

💡 Aurangzeb ruled for about 49 years (1658-1707), the longest reign of any Mughal emperor.
Q.No 38 HistoryHindi
मैसूर निम्नलिखित में से किस तट के लाभदायक व्यापार को नियंत्रित करता था?
A
बंबई
B
हुगली
C
कोरोमंडल
D
मालाबार
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The kingdom of Mysore controlled the lucrative trade of the Malabar coast (south-western India), an important source of pepper and spices, which brought it into conflict with the English.

A is incorrect: Bombay (the Konkan coast) was a Maratha/English sphere, not controlled by Mysore.

B is incorrect: Hooghly is on the eastern (Bengal) coast, far from Mysore's influence.

C is incorrect: The Coromandel coast (south-east) was dominated by the British and French at Madras/Pondicherry.

💡 Mysore's control of Malabar's pepper and sandalwood trade under Haidar Ali and Tipu Sultan was a key cause of the Anglo-Mysore Wars.
Q.No 39 HistoryHindi
857 के विद्रोह के बाद बहादुर शाह जफर को निम्नलिखित में से किस स्थान पर जेल भेजा गया था?
A
शंघाई
B
ढाका
C
रंगून
✓ Correct
D
अंडमान

Solution

Correct answer: C. After the Revolt of 1857, the last Mughal emperor Bahadur Shah Zafar was tried and exiled (imprisoned) to Rangoon in Burma, where he died in 1862.

A is incorrect: Shanghai (China) was never a place of his imprisonment.

B is incorrect: Dhaka was not where he was sent.

D is incorrect: The Andaman Islands (Cellular Jail) held many later freedom fighters, but Zafar was exiled to Rangoon.

💡 Bahadur Shah Zafar was also a noted Urdu poet; his tomb in Yangon (Rangoon) is a site of pilgrimage.
Q.No 38 HistoryEnglish
प्रथम अंग्रेज़-मैसूर युद्ध किस वर्ष में हुआ था?
A
1767-69
✓ Correct
B
1780-84
C
1790-92
D
1799

Solution

Correct answer: A. The First Anglo-Mysore War was fought in 1767-1769 between the British East India Company and Haidar Ali of Mysore, ending with the Treaty of Madras.

B is incorrect: 1780-84 was the Second Anglo-Mysore War.

C is incorrect: 1790-92 was the Third Anglo-Mysore War.

D is incorrect: 1799 was the Fourth (final) Anglo-Mysore War, in which Tipu Sultan died.

💡 Haidar Ali nearly captured Madras in the First War, forcing the British into the humiliating Treaty of Madras (1769).
Q.No 39 HistoryHindi
946 के प्रांतीय चुनावों के दौरान किस राजनीतिक दल ने "सामान्य" निर्वाचन क्षेत्रों में अच्छा प्रदर्शन किया था?
A
भारतीय राष्ट्रीय कांग्रेस
✓ Correct
B
जस्टिस पार्टी
C
भारतीय कम्युनिस्ट पार्टी
D
स्वराज पार्टी

Solution

Correct answer: A. In the 1946 provincial elections, the Indian National Congress performed very well in the 'general' (non-Muslim) constituencies, winning a large majority of those seats.

B is incorrect: The Justice Party had declined by 1946 and was not a major winner.

C is incorrect: The Communist Party of India won only a handful of seats.

D is incorrect: The Swaraj Party was a 1920s formation and no longer a separate force in 1946.

💡 The Muslim League won most of the Muslim-reserved seats in 1946, strengthening its demand for Pakistan.
Q.No 39 HistoryEnglish
Who among the following was never the Nawab of Bengal?
A
Alivardi Khan
B
Saadat Ali Khan
✓ Correct
C
Mir Qasim
D
Sirajuddaulah

Solution

Correct answer: B. Saadat Ali Khan was the Nawab of Awadh (Oudh), not of Bengal, so he was never the Nawab of Bengal.

A is incorrect: Alivardi Khan was Nawab of Bengal (1740-1756).

C is incorrect: Mir Qasim was Nawab of Bengal after Mir Jafar.

D is incorrect: Sirajuddaulah was the Nawab of Bengal defeated at the Battle of Plassey (1757).

💡 The Nawabs of Bengal in this era included Alivardi Khan, Sirajuddaulah, Mir Jafar and Mir Qasim.
Q.No 40 HistoryEnglish
Henry Lawrence was the Chief Commissioner of which state during the Revolt of 1857?
A
Awadh
✓ Correct
B
Odisha
C
Maharashtra
D
Punjab

Solution

Correct answer: A. Sir Henry Lawrence was the Chief Commissioner of Awadh (Oudh) during the Revolt of 1857 and died defending the Residency at Lucknow.

B is incorrect: Odisha was not the region of Henry Lawrence's command.

C is incorrect: Maharashtra was not under his charge during the revolt.

D is incorrect: Punjab was administered by his brother John Lawrence; Henry was posted to Awadh.

💡 The Siege of the Lucknow Residency was one of the most famous episodes of the 1857 Revolt.
Q.No 28 HistoryHindi
भारत के स्वतंत्रता संग्राम के दौरान किस युवा क्रांतिकारी ने 4 वर्ष की आयु में एक ब्रिटिश मजिस्ट्रेट की हत्या कर दी थी?
A
सुनीति चौधरी
✓ Correct
B
तारा रानी श्रीवास्तव
C
मातंगिनी हाजरा
D
अरुणा आसफ अली

Solution

Correct answer: A. Suniti Choudhury, a teenage revolutionary, was about 14 when she (with Shanti Ghosh) shot dead the British magistrate of Comilla in 1931.

B is incorrect: Tara Rani Srivastava was a Quit-India-movement activist, not the magistrate's assassin.

C is incorrect: Matangini Hazara was a Gandhian who died in the 1942 movement.

D is incorrect: Aruna Asaf Ali is famous for hoisting the flag during the 1942 Quit India Movement.

💡 Suniti Choudhury was among the youngest revolutionaries imprisoned in the Indian freedom struggle.
Q.No 38 HistoryHindi
लखनऊ में अंग्रेजों के ख़िलाफ़ 857 के विद्रोह को बढ़ावा देने में किसने बढ़ चढ़कर हिस्सा लिया था?
A
नाना साहब
B
बेगम हज़रत महल
✓ Correct
C
रानी लक्ष्मीबाई
D
बहादुर शाह ज़फ़र

Solution

Correct answer: B. Begum Hazrat Mahal led and inspired the 1857 revolt against the British in Lucknow (Awadh), fighting on behalf of her young son Birjis Qadr.

A is incorrect: Nana Saheb led the revolt at Kanpur, not Lucknow.

C is incorrect: Rani Lakshmibai led the uprising at Jhansi.

D is incorrect: Bahadur Shah Zafar was the symbolic leader at Delhi, not Lucknow.

💡 After the revolt failed, Begum Hazrat Mahal took refuge in Nepal, where she died in 1879.
Q.No 39 HistoryEnglish⚠ low OCR confidence
857 के विद्रोह के दौरान अंग्रेजों ने विद्रोहियों से दिल्‍ली का नियंत्रण कब वापस लिया था?
A
June 1857
B
September 1857
✓ Correct
C
March 1858
D
July 1858

Solution

Correct answer: B. The British recaptured Delhi from the rebels in September 1857, after a prolonged siege, ending the symbolic centre of the revolt.

A is incorrect: In June 1857 the revolt was still spreading; Delhi was in rebel hands.

C is incorrect: By March 1858 the focus had shifted to Lucknow's recapture.

D is incorrect: July 1858 is after Delhi had already fallen.

💡 After Delhi's fall, Bahadur Shah Zafar was captured and later exiled to Rangoon.
Q.No 39 HistoryEnglish
निम्नलिखित में से किस वर्ष के आस पास संथालों ने विद्रोह किया था?
A
1821
B
1855
✓ Correct
C
1885
D
1903

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Santhal rebellion (Santhal Hul) broke out around 1855 (1855-56) in present-day Jharkhand against oppressive moneylenders and British revenue officials.

A is incorrect: 1821 is too early for the Santhal rebellion.

C is incorrect: 1885 is the year the Indian National Congress was founded, not the Santhal revolt.

D is incorrect: 1903 is well after the Santhal uprising.

💡 The revolt was led by the brothers Sidhu and Kanhu Murmu.
Q.No 40 HistoryHindi
ईसाई मिशनरियों द्वारा स्थापित सेरामपुर कॉलेज किस नदी के तट पर स्थित था?
A
हुगली
✓ Correct
B
यमुना
C
दामोदर
D
महानंदा

Solution

Correct answer: A. Serampore College, founded by Christian missionaries (the 'Serampore Trio' - Carey, Marshman and Ward), stood on the bank of the Hooghly river in Bengal.

B is incorrect: The Yamuna flows through north India, not near Serampore.

C is incorrect: The Damodar is a different Bengal river, not the site of the college.

D is incorrect: The Mahananda is in north Bengal, not where Serampore College was founded.

💡 Serampore College (1818) was among the first institutions in Asia empowered to award university degrees.
Q.No 28 HistoryBengali
ইতিহাসে ভারতের প্রথম মহিলা শহিদ ও আত্মঘাতী হামলাকারী কে? তিনি রানি ভেলু নাচিয়ারের সেনা প্রধানও ছিলেন
A
কুয়িলি
✓ Correct
B
লক্ষ্মী শেহগাল
C
মাতঙ্গিনী হাজরা
D
ঝালকারি বাই

Solution

Correct answer: A. Kuyili (Velu Nachiyar's army commander) is regarded as India's first woman martyr / suicide attacker, who set herself ablaze to destroy a British arsenal.

B is incorrect: Lakshmi Sehgal led the INA's Rani of Jhansi regiment much later, in the 1940s.

C is incorrect: Matangini Hazra died in the 1942 Quit India Movement.

D is incorrect: Jhalkari Bai was a soldier in Rani Lakshmibai's army during 1857.

💡 Velu Nachiyar of Sivaganga was one of the earliest queens to wage war against the British (late 1700s).
Q.No 38 HistoryBengali
ব্রিটিশদের দ্বারা প্রবর্তিত চিরস্থায়ী বন্দোবস্তের অধীনে, জমিদারদের উপর প্রধান দায়িত্ব কী ছিল?
A
নিজেরাই জমি চাষ করবে
B
কৃষকদের কাছ থেকে খাজনা আদায় করা এবং কোম্পানিকে রাজস্ব প্রদান করা
✓ Correct
C
ব্রিটিশদের সাথে পণ্য বাণিজ্য
D
বাণিজ্য রুট পরিচালনা করা

Solution

Correct answer: B. Under the Permanent Settlement (introduced by Lord Cornwallis in 1793), the zamindars' chief duty was to collect rent from the peasants and pay a fixed revenue to the Company.

A is incorrect: Zamindars were intermediaries who collected rent, not cultivators of the land themselves.

C is incorrect: Trading goods with the British was not the zamindar's defined duty.

D is incorrect: Managing trade routes was not part of the Permanent Settlement obligations.

💡 The Permanent Settlement fixed the land revenue permanently, but harsh terms often ruined both zamindars and peasants.
Q.No 39 HistoryBengali
ব্রিটিশরা সাধারণত স্থানীয় ভাষা বা উপভাষা বোঝাতে কোন শব্দটি ব্যবহার করত?
A
আঞ্চলিক ভাষা
✓ Correct
B
Y J
C
ফেডারেল
D
ভাষাবিদ

Solution

Correct answer: A. The British generally used the term 'vernacular' to refer to the local language or dialect of the people.

B is incorrect: This option does not denote a local language.

C is incorrect: 'Federal' refers to a system of government, not a local language.

D is incorrect: A 'linguist' is a person who studies languages, not the term for the local tongue.

💡 The Vernacular Press Act of 1878 was used to muzzle Indian-language newspapers.
Q.No 39 HistoryEnglish
In which of the following years did the East India Company ultimately annexed Punjab?
A
1836
B
1841
C
1849
✓ Correct
D
1856

Solution

Correct answer: C. The East India Company annexed Punjab in 1849, after defeating the Sikhs in the Second Anglo-Sikh War.

A is incorrect: 1836 is too early; the Sikh kingdom under Ranjit Singh was still strong.

B is incorrect: 1841 pre-dates the annexation.

D is incorrect: 1856 was the year Awadh was annexed, not Punjab.

💡 After annexation, the famous Koh-i-Noor diamond was taken from the Lahore treasury to Britain.
Q.No 40 HistoryEnglish
in which of the following year the Court of Directors of the East India Company in London sent an educational (Wood's) dispatch to the Governor-General in India?
A
1830
B
1854
✓ Correct
C
1861
D
1875

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Court of Directors of the East India Company sent the educational despatch known as Wood's Despatch in 1854, often called the 'Magna Carta of English education in India'.

A is incorrect: 1830 is before the despatch was issued.

C is incorrect: 1861 is after Wood's Despatch.

D is incorrect: 1875 is unrelated to Wood's Despatch.

💡 Wood's Despatch led to the establishment of the universities of Calcutta, Bombay and Madras in 1857.
Q.No 29 HistoryEnglish
Which mosque within Agra Fort was constructed by Shah Jahan for the royal ladies and features three domes?
A
Moti Masjid
B
Mina Masjid
C
Nagina Masjid
✓ Correct
D
Jama Masjid

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Nagina Masjid inside the Agra Fort was built by Shah Jahan for the ladies of the royal household and has three domes.

A is incorrect: The Moti Masjid in Agra Fort is a larger pearl mosque, not the private ladies' mosque described.

B is incorrect: The Mina Masjid was a tiny private mosque, not the three-domed ladies' mosque.

D is incorrect: The Jama Masjid at Agra stands outside the fort and was built for the public.

💡 Shah Jahan, who built the Taj Mahal, was a great Mughal patron of marble architecture.
Q.No 37 HistoryEnglish
Which crop did the British force the peasants of Maharashtra to produce?
A
Cotton
✓ Correct
B
Tea
C
Jute
D
Barley

Solution

Correct answer: A. The British forced peasants of Maharashtra (the Deccan) to grow cotton, especially to feed Britain's textile mills during the American Civil War shortage.

B is incorrect: Tea was grown on plantations in Assam and the hills, not forced on Maharashtra's peasants.

C is incorrect: Jute cultivation was concentrated in Bengal.

D is incorrect: Barley was not the cash crop forced in Maharashtra.

💡 The cotton boom and bust contributed to peasant indebtedness behind the 1875 Deccan Riots.
Q.No 38 HistoryEnglish
in which year the World Parliament of Religions was held in Chicago?
A
1893
✓ Correct
B
1895
C
1897
D
1899

Solution

Correct answer: A. The World's Parliament of Religions was held in Chicago in 1893, where Swami Vivekananda delivered his famous address.

B is incorrect: 1895 is incorrect.

C is incorrect: 1897 is the year Vivekananda founded the Ramakrishna Mission, not the Chicago address.

D is incorrect: 1899 is after the event.

💡 Vivekananda began his 1893 speech with 'Sisters and Brothers of America', earning a standing ovation.
Q.No 39 HistoryHindi
निम्नलिखित में से किसके साथ मिलकर रानी लक्ष्मीबाई ने झाँसी में अंग्रेजों को भारी चुनौती दी थी?
A
रानी अवन्ति बाई लोधी
B
बहादुर शाह ज़फ़र
C
बेगम हज़रत महल
D
ताँत्या टोपे
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Rani Lakshmibai was strongly supported by Tatya Tope, who fought alongside her against the British at Jhansi and Gwalior in 1857-58.

A is incorrect: Rani Avantibai Lodhi led a separate revolt in Ramgarh.

B is incorrect: Bahadur Shah Zafar was the symbolic leader at Delhi.

C is incorrect: Begum Hazrat Mahal led the revolt at Lucknow.

💡 Tatya Tope was captured and executed in 1859; Rani Lakshmibai died fighting near Gwalior in 1858.
Q.No 40 HistoryHindi
भारत की स्वतंत्रता के लिए लड़ने के लिए सुभाष चंद्र बोस द्वारा गठित सेना का नाम क्या था?
A
स्वराज सेना
B
हिंदुस्तान लिबरेशन आर्मी
C
इंडियन नेशनल आर्मी (आई.एन.ए.)
✓ Correct
D
भारत मुक्ति सेना

Solution

Correct answer: C. Subhas Chandra Bose organised and led the Indian National Army (INA, Azad Hind Fauj) to fight for India's independence with the slogan 'Dilli Chalo'.

A is incorrect: 'Swaraj Sena' was not the name of Bose's army.

B is incorrect: 'Hindustan Liberation Army' is incorrect.

D is incorrect: 'Bharat Mukti Sena' is not the INA.

💡 The INA included the Rani of Jhansi Regiment, one of the first all-women combat units in Asia.
Q.No 37 HistoryHindi
निम्नलिखित में से किसे “शेर-ए-मैसूर” कहा जाता था?
A
टीपू सुल्तान
✓ Correct
B
हैदर अली
C
अलीवर्दी खान
D
महादजी शिंदे

Solution

Correct answer: A. Tipu Sultan was called the 'Sher-e-Mysore' (Tiger of Mysore) for his fierce resistance against the British.

B is incorrect: Haidar Ali was Tipu's father and predecessor, not the one called Tiger of Mysore.

C is incorrect: Alivardi Khan was the Nawab of Bengal.

D is incorrect: Mahadji Shinde was a Maratha leader.

💡 Tipu Sultan died in 1799 defending his capital Srirangapatna in the Fourth Anglo-Mysore War.
Q.No 38 HistoryEnglish
कांग्रेस और मुस्लिम लीग के बीच ऐतिहासिक लखनऊ समझौते पर किस वर्ष में दस्तखत हुए थे?
A
1929
B
1925
C
1919
D
1916
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The historic Lucknow Pact between the Indian National Congress and the Muslim League was signed in 1916, promoting Hindu-Muslim unity for self-government.

A is incorrect: 1929 is associated with the Nehru Report era, not the Lucknow Pact.

B is incorrect: 1925 is incorrect.

C is incorrect: 1919 was the year of the Rowlatt Act and Jallianwala Bagh, not the Pact.

💡 The Lucknow Pact was facilitated by leaders including Bal Gangadhar Tilak and Muhammad Ali Jinnah.
Q.No 37 HistoryHindi
प्राचीन भारत में जैन धर्म ने किस भाषा को अपनी पवित्र भाषा के रूप में अपनाया था?
A
उर्दू
B
प्राकृत
✓ Correct
C
तेलुगू
D
बंगाली

Solution

Correct answer: B. Jainism adopted Prakrit (especially Ardhamagadhi) as its sacred language; Jain scriptures (Agamas) were composed in Prakrit.

A is incorrect: Urdu developed much later and is unrelated to ancient Jain texts.

C is incorrect: Telugu is a Dravidian language, not the language of Jain scriptures.

D is incorrect: Bengali is a later regional language, not ancient Jain Prakrit.

💡 Buddhism similarly used Pali, another Prakrit, for its early texts.
Q.No 38 HistoryHindi
संगम युग के संदर्भ में, तीनों 'संगम' के संरक्षण में हुए था।
A
गुप्त राजाओं
B
पांड्य राजाओं
✓ Correct
C
मौर्य राजाओं
D
सातवाहन राजाओं

Solution

Correct answer: B. The three Sangams (assemblies of Tamil poets) of the Sangam Age were held under the patronage of the Pandya kings.

A is incorrect: The Gupta kings ruled in the north, unrelated to the Tamil Sangams.

C is incorrect: The Mauryan kings did not patronise the Sangams.

D is incorrect: The Satavahanas ruled the Deccan, not the Tamil Sangams.

💡 Sangam literature is the earliest body of Tamil writing, dating roughly from 300 BCE to 300 CE.
Q.No 39 HistoryHindi
पेशवाओं के अधीन मराठा साम्राज्य की राजधानी कौन सा शहर बना था?
A
मुंबई
B
नागपुर
C
पुणे
✓ Correct
D
औरंगाबाद

Solution

Correct answer: C. Pune became the capital of the Maratha Empire under the Peshwas, who made it the centre of their administration.

A is incorrect: Mumbai (Bombay) was a British/Portuguese trading centre, not the Peshwa capital.

B is incorrect: Nagpur was the seat of the Bhonsles, not the Peshwa capital.

D is incorrect: Aurangabad was a Mughal/Nizam centre.

💡 The Peshwas were hereditary prime ministers who became the effective rulers of the Maratha confederacy from Pune.
Q.No 21 HistoryHindi
गुरु गोबिंद सिंह जी द्वारा खालसा पंथ की स्थापना करने वाले पांच सिखों के नाम क्या हैं?
A
पंज प्यारे
✓ Correct
B
पंज पियारा
C
पंज सिख
D
पंज देव

Solution

Correct answer: A. The five Sikhs initiated by Guru Gobind Singh when he founded the Khalsa in 1699 are known as the 'Panj Pyare' (the Five Beloved Ones).

B is incorrect: 'Panj Piara' is just a misspelling, not the recognised term.

C is incorrect: 'Panj Sikh' is not the historical name.

D is incorrect: 'Panj Dev' is incorrect.

💡 The Khalsa was founded at Anandpur Sahib on Baisakhi in 1699.
Q.No 36 HistoryHindi
संगम युग के दौरान तमिल क्षेत्र पर किन महत्वपूर्ण सरदारों का प्रभुत्व था?
A
चालुक्य, चेर और मौर्य
B
चोल, चेर और पांड्य
✓ Correct
C
चोल, सातवाहन और गुप्त
D
पल्लव, मौर्य और नंद

Solution

Correct answer: B. During the Sangam Age the Tamil region was dominated by three important ruling chiefdoms - the Cholas, the Cheras and the Pandyas.

A is incorrect: Chalukyas and Mauryas were not Sangam-age Tamil chiefdoms.

C is incorrect: Satavahanas and Guptas were northern/Deccan powers.

D is incorrect: Pallavas, Mauryas and Nandas were not the Sangam-age Tamil trio.

💡 The emblems of the three dynasties were the tiger (Chola), the bow (Chera) and the fish (Pandya).
Q.No 37 HistoryHindi
काशी महाजनपद किस वर्तमान शहर से संबंधित है?
A
जयपुर
B
मथुरा
C
बनारस (वाराणसी)
✓ Correct
D
इलाहाबाद

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Kashi Mahajanapada corresponds to the present-day city of Banaras (Varanasi).

A is incorrect: Jaipur is a much later Rajput city.

B is incorrect: Mathura was a separate centre (Surasena Mahajanapada).

D is incorrect: Allahabad (Prayagraj) was not the Kashi Mahajanapada.

💡 Kashi was one of the sixteen Mahajanapadas (great kingdoms) of ancient India.
Q.No 38 HistoryHindi
स्वामी विवेकानंद के गुरु कौन थे?
A
रामानंद
B
आदि शंकराचार्य
C
चैतन्य महाप्रभु
D
रामकृष्ण परमहंस
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Swami Vivekananda's guru was Ramakrishna Paramhansa, the saint of Dakshineswar.

A is incorrect: Ramananda was a medieval Bhakti saint, not Vivekananda's guru.

B is incorrect: Adi Shankaracharya lived in the 8th century.

C is incorrect: Chaitanya Mahaprabhu was a 15th-16th century Vaishnava saint.

💡 Vivekananda founded the Ramakrishna Mission in 1897 in memory of his guru.
Q.No 39 HistoryHindi
निम्नलिखित नेताओं में से कौन भारत के विभाजन के सख्त ख़िलाफ थे?
A
मोहम्मद अली जिन्ना
B
चौधरी रहमत अली
C
लियाकत अली खान
D
खान अब्दुल गफ्फार खान
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan ('Frontier Gandhi') was strongly opposed to the Partition of India and worked for Hindu-Muslim unity.

A is incorrect: Muhammad Ali Jinnah led the demand for Pakistan.

B is incorrect: Chaudhry Rahmat Ali coined the name 'Pakistan'.

C is incorrect: Liaquat Ali Khan was a Muslim League leader who became Pakistan's first PM.

💡 Ghaffar Khan founded the non-violent 'Khudai Khidmatgar' (Red Shirts) movement among the Pashtuns.
Q.No 39 HistoryHindi
किस मुग़ल शासक ने साम्राज्य को मज़बूत किया और वह प्रशासनिक सुधारों और धार्मिक सहिष्णुता के लिए जाना जाता है?
A
अकबर
✓ Correct
B
जहांगीर
C
औरंगजेब
D
हुमायूं

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Mughal emperor Akbar strengthened the empire and is famous for his administrative reforms and policy of religious tolerance (sulh-i-kul).

B is incorrect: Jahangir is known more for art and justice than for major administrative reforms.

C is incorrect: Aurangzeb is associated with expansion but also religious orthodoxy, not tolerance.

D is incorrect: Humayun lost and regained the throne but did not consolidate it like Akbar.

💡 Akbar introduced the Mansabdari system and the Din-i-Ilahi, and abolished the jizya tax.
Q.No 32 HistoryEnglish
Aurangzeb’s long Deccan campaigns mainly aimed at subduing
A
The Rajputs
B
The Marathas
✓ Correct
C
The Afghans
D
The Portuguese

Solution

Correct answer: B. Aurangzeb's long Deccan campaigns were mainly aimed at subduing the Marathas, who waged persistent guerrilla resistance.

A is incorrect: He fought the Rajputs earlier, but the Deccan wars targeted the Marathas.

C is incorrect: The Afghans were a frontier concern, not the focus of the Deccan campaigns.

D is incorrect: The Portuguese held coastal pockets but were not the main target.

💡 The exhausting Deccan wars drained the Mughal treasury and contributed to the empire's decline.
Q.No 34 HistoryEnglish
Which Pallava king defeated and killed Pulakesin II?
A
Dhruva
B
Parantaka |
C
Narasimhavarman |
✓ Correct
D
Amoghavarsha |

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Pallava king Narasimhavarman I defeated and killed the Chalukya ruler Pulakesin II (around 642 CE) and captured the Chalukya capital Vatapi.

A is incorrect: Dhruva was a Rashtrakuta king.

B is incorrect: Parantaka I was a Chola king.

D is incorrect: Amoghavarsha I was a Rashtrakuta king.

💡 For this victory Narasimhavarman I took the title 'Vatapikonda' (conqueror of Vatapi).
Q.No 37 HistoryEnglish
Who delivered a speech in Bombay on January 15, 1908, declaring that the purpose of national education was to awaken the spirit of nationalism among students?
A
Rabindranath Tagore
B
Sri Aurobindo Ghose
✓ Correct
C
Lala Lajpat Rai
D
Mahatma Gandhi

Solution

Correct answer: B. Sri Aurobindo Ghose delivered a speech in Bombay on 15 January 1908 stressing that national education should awaken the spirit of nationalism in students.

A is incorrect: Rabindranath Tagore promoted education (Santiniketan) but did not give this Bombay speech.

C is incorrect: Lala Lajpat Rai was an extremist leader but not the speaker here.

D is incorrect: Mahatma Gandhi was still in South Africa in 1908.

💡 Sri Aurobindo was a leading figure of the Swadeshi and national-education movements before turning to spirituality.
Q.No 36 HistoryHindi
मुगल साम्राज्य के अंतर्गत ज़ब्त प्रणाली गुजरात तथा बंगाल जैसे प्रांतों में क्यों लागू नहीं की गई थी?
A
मिट्टी की खराब गुणवत्ता
B
लगातार विदेशी आक्रमण
C
भूमि का निरीक्षण करने में असमर्थता
✓ Correct
D
स्थानीय प्रशासन की कमी

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Zabt revenue system was not applied in provinces like Gujarat and Bengal mainly because of the inability to survey and measure the land accurately there.

A is incorrect: Poor soil quality was not the reason for skipping Zabt.

B is incorrect: Continuous foreign invasions were not the cause.

D is incorrect: It was not due to a lack of local administration as such, but the difficulty of land measurement.

💡 The Zabt system, developed under Akbar (Todar Mal), fixed revenue based on measured land and average yields.
Q.No 31 HistoryHindi
शंकरदेव की शिक्षाएँ मुख्य रूप से किस पवित्र ग्रंथ पर आधारित हैं? ।. भगवद गीता |. भागवत पुराण
A
Only I
B
Only II
C
Both I and II
✓ Correct
D
Neither I nor II

Solution

Correct answer: C. Shankardev's teachings (Ekasarana Dharma) drew mainly on both the Bhagavad Gita (I) and the Bhagavata Purana (II).

A is incorrect: 'Only I' leaves out the Bhagavata Purana.

B is incorrect: 'Only II' leaves out the Bhagavad Gita.

D is incorrect: 'Neither' is wrong since both texts influenced him.

💡 Srimanta Shankardev was a 15th-16th century Assamese saint who founded the Neo-Vaishnavite movement.
Q.No 33 HistoryHindi
'उपनिषद' शब्द का शाब्दिक अर्थ है |
A
ऊँची आवाज़ में बोलना
B
अच्छी लिखावट में लिखना
C
चुपचाप ध्यान करना
D
पास में ही नीचे बैठना
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The word 'Upanishad' literally means 'to sit down near' (a teacher) - referring to disciples sitting close to a guru to receive secret knowledge.

A is incorrect: It does not mean speaking loudly.

B is incorrect: It does not mean writing in good handwriting.

C is incorrect: It does not mean meditating silently, though learning was reflective.

💡 The Upanishads form the concluding part of the Vedas and are known as 'Vedanta'.
Q.No 37 HistoryHindi
निम्नलिखित में से किस स्थान पर हुई बैठक में हिंदुस्तान सोशलिस्ट रिपब्लिकन आर्मी (एच.एस.आर.ए.) की स्थापना की गई थी?
A
बॉम्बे के गोकुलदास तेजपाल संस्कृत महाविद्यालय में
B
दिल्‍ली के फिरोजशाह कोटला मैदान में
✓ Correct
C
इलाहाबाद के अल्फ्रेड पार्क में
D
अमृतसर के जलियांवाला बाग में

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA) was founded at a meeting held at the Ferozshah Kotla ground in Delhi (1928).

A is incorrect: The Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay hosted the first Congress session (1885), not the HSRA founding.

C is incorrect: Alfred Park, Allahabad is where Chandrashekhar Azad died, not where HSRA was founded.

D is incorrect: Jallianwala Bagh, Amritsar is associated with the 1919 massacre.

💡 The HSRA's members included Bhagat Singh, Chandrashekhar Azad and Sukhdev.
Q.No 36 HistoryHindi
मुगल साम्राज्य के अंतर्गत मनसबदार की सैन्य जिम्मेदारी क्या थी?
A
घुड़सवारों की एक निश्चित संख्या बनाए रखना
✓ Correct
B
कर एकत्रित करना
C
न्याय का प्रशासन करना
D
भूमि वितरण की देखरेख करना

Solution

Correct answer: A. Under the Mughal mansabdari system, a mansabdar's military responsibility was to maintain a fixed number of horsemen (sawars) ready for the emperor's service.

B is incorrect: Collecting taxes was the job of revenue officials, not the core military duty.

C is incorrect: Administering justice was a separate function.

D is incorrect: Overseeing land distribution was not the mansabdar's defined military duty.

💡 A mansabdar's rank had two numbers - 'zat' (personal status) and 'sawar' (cavalry to be maintained).
Q.No 29 HistoryHindi
अर्थशास्त्र, जिसे पारंपरिक रूप से चाणक्य से जोड़ा जाता है, मुख्य रूप से से संबंधित है।
A
कविता और साहित्य
B
धार्मिक अनुष्ठान
C
शासन कला और प्रशासन
✓ Correct
D
खगोल

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Arthashastra, traditionally attributed to Chanakya (Kautilya), deals mainly with statecraft, economics and administration.

A is incorrect: It is not a work of poetry and literature.

B is incorrect: It is not about religious rituals.

D is incorrect: It is not a treatise on astronomy.

💡 Chanakya was the mentor of Chandragupta Maurya, founder of the Mauryan Empire.
Q.No 31 HistoryHindi
सल्तनत के अधीन इक्‍्ता प्रणाली मध्ययुगीन यूरोप की सामंती प्रणाली से मुख्यतः: इसलिए मिलती जुलती है क्योंकि ।
A
दोनों में भूमि के वंशानुगत हस्तांतरण की अनुमति दी जाती है
B
दोनों को राजस्व अधिकारों के बदले सैन्य सेवा के लिए डिज़ाइन किया गया था
✓ Correct
C
दोनों व्यापारिक एकाधिकार थे
D
दोनों व्यापारियों पर लागू होने वाली कराधान की प्रणालियाँ थीं

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Iqta system under the Delhi Sultanate resembled European feudalism mainly because both granted revenue rights (over land) in return for military service.

A is incorrect: The Iqta was not originally hereditary, unlike much European feudal land.

C is incorrect: Neither was a trade monopoly.

D is incorrect: They were not merely taxation systems on merchants.

💡 An 'iqtadar' (or muqti) collected revenue from his iqta and maintained troops for the Sultan.
Q.No 32 HistoryHindi
किस अधिनियम ने पहली बार भारतीयों को केन्द्रीय विधान सभा में प्रतिनिधित्व दिया?
A
Indian Councils Act, 1861
B
Indian Councils Act, 1892
C
Indian Councils Act, 1909
✓ Correct
D
Government of India Act, 1919

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Indian Councils Act, 1909 (Morley-Minto Reforms) gave Indians a measure of representation in the central legislature for the first time.

A is incorrect: The 1861 Act allowed only nominated non-officials.

B is incorrect: The 1892 Act expanded councils but is not credited with this representation milestone.

D is incorrect: The 1919 Act came later.

💡 The 1909 Act also introduced separate electorates for Muslims, a controversial step.
Q.No 22 HistoryHindi
किस महाकाव्य में उल्लेख है कि उत्तरायण के दौरान मरने से मोक्ष की प्राप्ति होती है?
A
रामायण
B
महाभारत
✓ Correct
C
भागवत पुराण
D
वेद

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Mahabharata mentions that dying during Uttarayana (the sun's northward journey) leads to moksha - Bhishma waited for Uttarayana to give up his life.

A is incorrect: The Ramayana does not contain this Uttarayana-moksha idea prominently.

C is incorrect: The Bhagavata Purana is not the source of this specific belief.

D is incorrect: The Vedas are hymns/rituals, not the source of this narrative.

💡 Bhishma had the boon of 'ichha-mrityu' (death at will) and chose to die after Uttarayana began.
Q.No 31 HistoryHindi
दिल्‍ली सल्तनत में, सभी मुसलमानों पर लागू धार्मिक करो में से एक था।
A
ममलुक
B
जकात
✓ Correct
C
किस्मत
D
उर

Solution

Correct answer: B. Zakat was a religious tax levied on all Muslims (a form of obligatory charity) during the Delhi Sultanate.

A is incorrect: 'Mamluk' refers to slave soldiers/the slave dynasty, not a tax.

C is incorrect: 'Kismat' is not a Sultanate tax.

D is incorrect: 'Ushr' was a land tax on Muslims' produce, but the religious tax on all Muslims here is zakat.

💡 Non-Muslims under the Sultanate paid the jizya tax instead.
Q.No 27 HistoryEnglish
The Salt Law under the British administration was criticized because it
A
Monopolized salt production and imposed heavy taxes
✓ Correct
B
Encouraged private salt industries
C
Allowed Indians free access to salt pans
D
Abolished traditional salt-making methods

Solution

Correct answer: A. The British Salt Law was criticised because it gave the government a monopoly over salt production and imposed heavy taxes on this essential commodity.

B is incorrect: It suppressed, rather than encouraged, private salt industries.

C is incorrect: It denied, not allowed, Indians free access to salt.

D is incorrect: It did not abolish traditional methods to benefit Indians; it monopolised salt.

💡 Gandhi's 1930 Dandi Salt March was a direct protest against the salt tax.
Q.No 28 HistoryEnglish
Alvars saints were devoted to which of the following?
A
Durga
B
Vishnu
✓ Correct
C
Agni
D
Indra

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Alvar saints of South India were devoted to Lord Vishnu and composed devotional hymns in Tamil.

A is incorrect: Devotion to Durga is not associated with the Alvars.

C is incorrect: Agni is a Vedic deity, not the Alvars' focus.

D is incorrect: Indra is a Vedic deity, not worshipped by the Alvars.

💡 The Nayanars were the corresponding Shaiva (Shiva-devotee) saints of the same Bhakti tradition.
Q.No 30 HistoryEnglish
Which of the following objectives emphasizes ethical living in the Vedic framework?
A
Artha
B
Kama
C
Dharma
✓ Correct
D
Moksha

Solution

Correct answer: C. In the Vedic framework of the four 'purusharthas', Dharma emphasises ethical and righteous living.

A is incorrect: Artha concerns wealth and material prosperity.

B is incorrect: Kama concerns desires and pleasures.

D is incorrect: Moksha is liberation, the ultimate spiritual goal.

💡 The four purusharthas are Dharma, Artha, Kama and Moksha.
Q.No 27 HistoryHindi
निम्नलिखित में से कौन सल्तनत काल का एक प्रसिद्ध इतिहासकार था?
A
अमीर खुसरो
B
अबुल फ़ज़ल
C
अल-बिरूनी
D
ज़ियाउद्दीन बरनी
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Ziauddin Barani was a famous historian of the Delhi Sultanate, author of the 'Tarikh-i-Firoz Shahi'.

A is incorrect: Amir Khusrau was a poet and musician of the Sultanate, not primarily a historian of this kind.

B is incorrect: Abul Fazl was the historian of Akbar (Mughal), author of 'Akbarnama'.

C is incorrect: Al-Biruni was a scholar who came with Mahmud of Ghazni, before the Sultanate.

💡 Barani's work is a key source for the reigns of the Tughlaq sultans.
Q.No 25 HistoryHindi
दिल्‍ली सल्तनत के आखिरी शासक वंश, लोदी वंश का संस्थापक कौन था?
A
सिकंदर लोदी
B
इब्राहिम लोदी
C
बहलूल लोदी
✓ Correct
D
नासिर-उद-दीन लोदी

Solution

Correct answer: C. Bahlul Lodi founded the Lodi dynasty, the last ruling house of the Delhi Sultanate.

A is incorrect: Sikandar Lodi was Bahlul's son and successor.

B is incorrect: Ibrahim Lodi was the last Lodi sultan, defeated at Panipat (1526).

D is incorrect: 'Nasir-ud-din Lodi' was not the founder.

💡 Ibrahim Lodi's defeat by Babur at the First Battle of Panipat (1526) began Mughal rule.
Q.No 26 HistoryHindi
साइमन कमीशन (927) का बहिष्कार क्यों किया गया?
A
क्योंकि इसमें भारतीय सदस्यों को शामिल नहीं किया गया था।
✓ Correct
B
क्योंकि इसने प्रांतीय स्वायत्तता की अनुशंसा की थी।
C
क्योंकि इसने स्वतंत्र उपनिवेश का प्रस्ताव रखा था।
D
क्योंकि इसने पृथक निर्वाचिका को समाप्त कर दिया था।

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Simon Commission (1927) was boycotted because it had no Indian members - it was an all-British commission deciding India's constitutional future.

B is incorrect: It was not boycotted for recommending provincial autonomy.

C is incorrect: It did not propose independent colony status.

D is incorrect: It did not abolish separate electorates.

💡 Protesters greeted it with 'Simon Go Back'; Lala Lajpat Rai was injured in a related protest.
Q.No 30 HistoryHindi
कौन सा शहर पांड्य राजाओं की राजधानी था?
A
उज्जैन
B
वैशाली
C
अमरावती
D
मदुरे
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Madurai was the capital of the Pandya kings of ancient Tamil Nadu.

A is incorrect: Ujjain was a Malwa/Avanti capital.

B is incorrect: Vaishali was a Lichchhavi/Vajji capital.

C is incorrect: Amaravati was associated with the Satavahanas.

💡 Madurai's Meenakshi Amman Temple is one of the most famous temples in South India.
Q.No 22 HistoryHindi
कोणत्या हिंदू महाकाव्यात दसन्याशी संबंधित कथा सांगितली आहे?
A
महाभारत
B
रामायण
✓ Correct
C
भगवद्‌ गीता
D
वेद

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Ramayana narrates the story connected with Dussehra (Vijayadashami) - Lord Rama's victory over the demon king Ravana.

A is incorrect: The Mahabharata is a different epic, not the basis of Dussehra's central story.

C is incorrect: The Bhagavad Gita is part of the Mahabharata, not the Dussehra-Ravana story.

D is incorrect: The Vedas are hymns and rituals, not this narrative.

💡 Dussehra marks the triumph of good over evil; effigies of Ravana are burnt on this day.
Q.No 27 HistoryHindi
खालीलपैकी कोणत्या राण्यांनी तिच्या शिलालेखांवर तिचे नाव बदलले आणि पुरुष असल्याचे भासवले?
A
कित्तूर चेन्नम्मा
B
रुद्रमादेवी
✓ Correct
C
चांद बीबी
D
दुर्गावती

Solution

Correct answer: B. Rani Rudramadevi of the Kakatiya dynasty changed her name on her inscriptions and was portrayed as a man (Rudradeva) to rule more easily in a patriarchal era.

A is incorrect: Kittur Chennamma was a 19th-century queen who fought the British, not known for this.

C is incorrect: Chand Bibi defended Ahmednagar against the Mughals.

D is incorrect: Rani Durgavati of Gondwana fought the Mughals.

💡 Rudramadevi was one of the few women to rule a major South Indian kingdom in her own right.
Q.No 26 HistoryHindi
चोल अभिलेखों में उल्लिखित भूमि की विभिन्न श्रेणियों के संदर्भ में निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा युग्म सही है? ।. शालाभोग - विद्यालय के रखरखाव के लिए भूमि। |. देवदान, तिरुनमट्रक्कनी - मंदिरों को उपहार में दी गई भूमि।
A
Only I
B
Only II
C
Both I and II
✓ Correct
D
Neither I nor II

Solution

Correct answer: C. Both pairs are correct: Shalabhoga (I) was land for the maintenance of a school, and Devadana/Tirunamattukkani (II) was land gifted to temples - so 'Both I and II' is right.

A is incorrect: 'Only I' omits the correct temple-land term.

B is incorrect: 'Only II' omits the correct school-land term.

D is incorrect: 'Neither' is wrong as both pairs are correctly matched.

💡 Chola inscriptions record several land categories, reflecting a well-organised rural economy.
Q.No 28 HistoryHindi
प्रसिद्ध कांस्य 'नृत्य करती लड़की' मूर्ति में खोजी गई थी।
A
लोथल
B
चन्हूदड़ो
C
मोहनजोदड़ो
✓ Correct
D
रोपड़

Solution

Correct answer: C. The famous bronze 'Dancing Girl' figurine was discovered at Mohenjo-daro, a major Indus Valley Civilization site.

A is incorrect: Lothal was a dockyard town but not where the Dancing Girl was found.

B is incorrect: Chanhudaro is known for bead-making.

D is incorrect: Ropar (Rupnagar) is an Indus site but not where this figure was found.

💡 The Dancing Girl, made by the lost-wax casting method, shows the metallurgical skill of the Harappans.
Q.No 26 HistoryHindi
निम्नलिखित में से कौन अकबर का राजस्व मंत्री था जिसे भूमि राजस्व सुधारों का श्रेय दिया जाता है?
A
अबुल फ़ज़ल
B
बीरबल
C
राजा टोडरमल
✓ Correct
D
तानसैन

Solution

Correct answer: C. Raja Todarmal was Akbar's revenue minister, credited with major land-revenue reforms (the 'Dahsala'/Zabt system).

A is incorrect: Abul Fazl was Akbar's chronicler (Akbarnama).

B is incorrect: Birbal was a witty courtier and adviser.

D is incorrect: Tansen was the legendary court musician.

💡 Todarmal's system measured land and fixed revenue based on average yields and prices.
Q.No 21 HistoryHindi
699 में वैसाखी के दिन कौन-सी महत्वपूर्ण घटना घटी?
A
गुरु नानक देव जी का जन्म हुआ
B
पहला सिख मंदिर बनाया गया
C
गुरु गोबिंद सिंह जी ने खालसा पंथ की शुरुआत की
✓ Correct
D
कुरान अवतरित हुआ

Solution

Correct answer: C. On Vaisakhi in 1699, Guru Gobind Singh founded the Khalsa Panth at Anandpur Sahib, initiating the Panj Pyare.

A is incorrect: Guru Nanak Dev was born in 1469, not on this Vaisakhi.

B is incorrect: The founding of the Khalsa, not the first Sikh temple, is the event.

D is incorrect: The revelation of the Quran is unrelated to Vaisakhi 1699.

💡 The Khalsa introduced the five Ks and the names 'Singh' and 'Kaur'.
Q.No 31 HistoryHindi
किस चालुक्य शासक ने नर्मदा नदी के तट पर हर्षवर्धन को हराया था?
A
राजेंद्र प्रथम
B
पुलकेशिन द्वितीय
✓ Correct
C
कृष्ण तृतीय
D
राजसिंह

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Chalukya ruler Pulakeshin II defeated Harshavardhana on the banks of the Narmada river.

A is incorrect: Rajendra I was a Chola king.

C is incorrect: Krishna III was a Rashtrakuta king.

D is incorrect: 'Rajasimha' is not the ruler who defeated Harsha here.

💡 This victory halted Harsha's southward expansion and is recorded in the Aihole inscription.
Q.No 35 HistoryEnglish
किस वर्ष सतारा को विलय नीति के तहत अधिग्रहित कर लिया गया था?
A
1842
B
1844
C
1846
D
1848
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Satara was annexed in 1848 under Lord Dalhousie's Doctrine of Lapse.

A is incorrect: 1842 is incorrect.

B is incorrect: 1844 is not the year.

C is incorrect: 1846 is incorrect.

💡 The Doctrine of Lapse annexed states whose rulers died without a natural heir, including Jhansi and Nagpur.
Q.No 24 HistoryEnglish
in which year Rajaraja | became the king of the Chola Empire?
A
985
✓ Correct
B
1008
C
1028
D
1045

Solution

Correct answer: A. Rajaraja I became the king of the Chola Empire in 985 CE.

B is incorrect: 1008 falls within his reign, not its start.

C is incorrect: 1028 is after his reign.

D is incorrect: 1045 is much later.

💡 Rajaraja I built the great Brihadisvara Temple at Thanjavur.
Q.No 29 HistoryOdia
ତାମିଲ ଭକ୍ତି ସାହିତ୍ୟରେ ବୈଷ୍ଣବ ସନ୍ଥ୍ରମାନଙ୍କୁ କୁହାଯାଉଥିଲା ।
A
ଯୋଗୀ
B
ଅଲବର
✓ Correct
C
ନୟନାର
D
ତୀଥଙ୍କର

Solution

Correct answer: B. In Tamil Bhakti literature, the Vaishnava saints (devotees of Vishnu) were called the Alvars.

A is incorrect: 'Yogi' is a general term, not the name of these saints.

C is incorrect: The Nayanars were the Shaiva (Shiva-devotee) saints.

D is incorrect: Tirthankaras are the spiritual teachers of Jainism.

💡 The Alvars and Nayanars led the Bhakti movement in South India.
Q.No 34 HistoryOdia
ବିର୍ସା ଏକ ମୁଣ୍ଡା ପରିବାରରେ ଜନ୍ମଗ୍ରହଣ କରିଥିଲେ, ଏକ ଆଦିବାସୀ ଗୋଷ୍ଠୀ ଯାହା ରେ ରହୁଥିଲେ ।
A
ଧର
B
ଜାବୁଆ
C
WIMAIG
D
ଛୋଟନାଗପୁର
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Birsa Munda was born into a Munda tribal family that lived in the Chotanagpur (plateau) region.

A is incorrect: 'Dhar' is not the region associated with Birsa Munda.

B is incorrect: Jhabua is in Madhya Pradesh, not Birsa's region.

C is incorrect: This option does not name his region.

💡 Birsa Munda led the 'Ulgulan' (Great Tumult) tribal rebellion against the British; his birth anniversary is Janjatiya Gaurav Divas.
Q.No 25 HistoryHindi
किस मुगलकालीन स्मारक को अक्सर "बेबी ताज" कहा जाता है और यह सफेद संगमरमर वास्तुकला की ओर एक शैलीगत बदलाव का प्रतीक है?
A
एत्मादुद्दौला का मकबरा
✓ Correct
B
बीबी का मकबरा
C
मोती मस्जिद
D
हुमायूँ का मकबरा

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Tomb of Itmad-ud-Daula in Agra is often called the 'Baby Taj'; it marked the shift towards white-marble Mughal architecture.

B is incorrect: Bibi Ka Maqbara (Aurangabad) is called the 'Mini Taj', a later imitation.

C is incorrect: The Moti Masjid is a mosque, not this tomb.

D is incorrect: Humayun's Tomb is an earlier red-sandstone structure.

💡 Itmad-ud-Daula's tomb was built by Nur Jahan for her father; it pioneered pietra dura inlay.
Q.No 28 HistoryHindi
चोल काल की कृषि में कावेरी नदी की नहरों की मुख्य भूमिका कया थी?
A
वे फसलों के लिए उपजाऊ मिट्टी तथा आर््रता प्रदान करती थीं।
✓ Correct
B
वे आक्रमणों से सुरक्षा प्रदान करती थीं।
C
वे व्यापार के लिए माल का परिवहन करती थीं।
D
उनका उपयोग मछली पकड़ने तथा नाव परिवहन के लिए किया जाता था।

Solution

Correct answer: A. In Chola-era agriculture, the canals from the Kaveri river provided fertile silt and moisture to the fields, supporting prosperous farming.

B is incorrect: They were for irrigation, not military protection.

C is incorrect: They were primarily for irrigation, not goods transport.

D is incorrect: Their main role was irrigation, not fishing/boat transport.

💡 The fertile Kaveri delta was the heartland of the Chola economy.
Q.No 26 HistoryHindi
बादामी के चालुक्य , भारतीय कला में किस योगदान के लिए जाने जाते हैं?
A
नटराज की कांस्य मूर्ति के लिए
B
बादामी में चट्टानों को काटकर बनाए गए गुफा मंदिर के लिए
✓ Correct
C
नागर शैली के मंदिरों का विकास करने के लिए
D
एलोरा गुफाओं में चित्रकारी के लिए

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Chalukyas of Badami are known for the rock-cut cave temples at Badami, an important contribution to Indian temple architecture.

A is incorrect: The bronze Nataraja is associated with the Cholas.

C is incorrect: The Nagara temple style developed mainly in the north.

D is incorrect: The Ellora paintings are linked to other dynasties (Rashtrakutas).

💡 Badami, Aihole and Pattadakal form the cradle of Chalukyan temple architecture.
Q.No 28 HistoryHindi
निम्नलिखित में से किस अधिनियम द्वारा पहली बार भारतीयों को लंदन में खुली प्रतियोगिता के माध्यम से भारतीय सिविल सेवा में भर्ती करने की अनुमति दी गई?
A
Charter Act of 1813
B
Charter Act of 1833
C
Charter Act of 1853
✓ Correct
D
Indian Councils Act of 1861

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Charter Act of 1853 first allowed Indians to be recruited to the Indian Civil Service through open competition held in London.

A is incorrect: The 1813 Charter Act dealt with the Company's monopoly and education funds.

B is incorrect: The 1833 Charter Act centralised administration under the Governor-General.

D is incorrect: The 1861 Act dealt with legislative councils.

💡 The first Indian to qualify for the ICS was Satyendranath Tagore in 1863.
Q.No 37 HistoryHindi
949 में थल सेनाध्यक्ष तथा बाद में फील्ड मार्शल बनने वाले पहले भारतीय कौन थे?
A
जनरल पी. एन. थापर
B
जनरल के. एम. करिअप्पा
✓ Correct
C
जनरल के. एस. थिमय्या
D
जनरल एस. एम. श्रीनागेश

Solution

Correct answer: B. General K.M. Cariappa became the first Indian Commander-in-Chief of the Army in 1949 and was later made a Field Marshal.

A is incorrect: P.N. Thapar was a later Army Chief.

C is incorrect: K.S. Thimayya was a later Army Chief.

D is incorrect: S.M. Shrinagesh was a later Army Chief.

💡 India has had only two Field Marshals: K.M. Cariappa and Sam Manekshaw.
Q.No 23 HistoryHindi
ह्लेन त्सांग (५08280) का विवरण विशेष रूप से के अध्ययन के लिए मूल्यवान है।
A
हड़प्पा नगर
B
हर्षवर्धन के दरबार
✓ Correct
C
मौर्यकालीन राजनीति
D
चोल नौसैनिक खोजयात्राओं

Solution

Correct answer: B. Hiuen Tsang's (Xuanzang's) accounts are especially valuable for studying the court of Harshavardhana, whom he visited in the 7th century.

A is incorrect: He did not describe Harappan towns (a far earlier civilization).

C is incorrect: He is not the main source for Mauryan polity (that is Megasthenes/Kautilya).

D is incorrect: He did not record Chola naval expeditions.

💡 Hiuen Tsang also studied at the Nalanda university during his stay in India.
Q.No 26 HistoryHindi
वर्ष 83 के चार्टर अधिनियम ने पर ईस्ट इंडिया कंपनी के एकाधिकार को समाप्त कर दिया।
A
अफीम व्यापार
B
चीन के साथ व्यापार
C
यूरोप के साथ व्यापार
D
चाय और चीन के व्यापार को छोड़कर भारत के साथ होने वाले व्यापार
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The Charter Act of 1813 ended the East India Company's trade monopoly over India, except for the tea trade and trade with China.

A is incorrect: It did not specifically end an opium-trade monopoly here.

B is incorrect: It retained the China-trade monopoly.

C is incorrect: It was about Indian trade, not just Europe.

💡 The 1833 Charter Act later ended even the China-tea monopoly, turning the Company into an administrator.
Q.No 28 HistoryHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
गुर्जर-प्रतिहार हरिचंद्र को किस क्षेत्र में राजशाही कायम करने के लिए जाना जाता हैं? [. तमिलनाडु . राजस्थान
A
Only I (Tamil Nadu)
B
Only II (Rajasthan)
✓ Correct
C
Both I and II
D
Neither I nor II

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Gurjara-Pratihara ruler Harichandra is known for establishing the dynasty's kingship in the Rajasthan region (II), so 'Only II' is correct.

A is incorrect: 'Only I' (Tamil Nadu) is wrong; the Pratiharas rose in the north-west.

C is incorrect: 'Both' is wrong since Tamil Nadu is not associated with him.

D is incorrect: 'Neither' is wrong since Rajasthan is correct.

💡 The Gurjara-Pratiharas were a major power in northern India and resisted Arab expansion.
Q.No 22 HistoryHindi
वल्लभी साम्राज्य, जिसने गुप्तों के बाद पश्चिमी भारत पर शासन किया, वर्तमान के में स्थित था।
A
राजस्थान
B
गुजरात
✓ Correct
C
महाराष्ट्र
D
मध्य प्रदेश

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Vallabhi kingdom, which ruled western India after the Guptas, was located in present-day Gujarat.

A is incorrect: It was not in Rajasthan.

C is incorrect: It was not in Maharashtra.

D is incorrect: It was not in Madhya Pradesh.

💡 Vallabhi was a noted centre of learning, with a university rivalling Nalanda.
Q.No 22 HistoryHindi
गुप्त साम्राज्य के पतन के बाद उत्तर भारत में कौन-सी क्षेत्रीय शक्ति प्रमुखता से उभरी?
A
चोल
B
वाकाटक
C
पुष्यभूति
✓ Correct
D
चेर

Solution

Correct answer: C. After the decline of the Gupta Empire, the Pushyabhuti dynasty (of Harshavardhana) rose to prominence in North India.

A is incorrect: The Cholas ruled the far south.

B is incorrect: The Vakatakas were earlier contemporaries of the Guptas in the Deccan.

D is incorrect: The Cheras ruled in the south (Kerala region).

💡 Harshavardhana of the Pushyabhuti dynasty made Kannauj a great centre of power.
Q.No 24 HistoryHindi
किस अधिनियम ने पाकिस्तान के निर्माण के लिए कानूनी आधार प्रदान किया ?
A
कैबिनेट मिशन योजना
B
भारतीय स्वतंत्रता अधिनियम, 947
✓ Correct
C
भारत सरकार अधिनियम, 935
D
माउंटबेटन योजना

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Indian Independence Act, 1947 provided the legal basis for the creation of Pakistan and the partition of India.

A is incorrect: The Cabinet Mission Plan (1946) proposed a united India framework.

C is incorrect: The Government of India Act, 1935 was a colonial constitutional act.

D is incorrect: The Mountbatten Plan was the proposal; the legal basis was the 1947 Act.

💡 The Act created the two Dominions of India and Pakistan on 15 August 1947.
Q.No 26 HistoryHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
अकबरनामा की तीसरी जिल्द को निम्नलिखित में से किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
A
तुज़ुक-ए-बाबुरी
B
रज़्मनामा
C
फ़तवा-ए-जहाँदारी
D
आइने-अकबरी
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The third volume of the Akbarnama is known as the 'Ain-i-Akbari', a detailed account of Akbar's administration.

A is incorrect: Tuzuk-e-Baburi is Babur's memoir.

B is incorrect: Razmnama is the Persian translation of the Mahabharata.

C is incorrect: Fatawa-i-Jahandari was written by Ziauddin Barani.

💡 The Akbarnama was written by Abul Fazl, Akbar's court historian.
Q.No 21 HistoryHindi
मान्यताओं के अनुसार, प्रसिद्ध संस्कृत कवि कालिदास के दरबार में फले-फूले थे।
A
स्कंदगुप्त
B
समुद्रगुप्त
C
चन्द्रगुप्त द्वितीय
✓ Correct
D
कुमारगुप्त

Solution

Correct answer: C. According to tradition, the great Sanskrit poet Kalidasa flourished in the court of Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya).

A is incorrect: Skandagupta was a later Gupta ruler.

B is incorrect: Samudragupta was Chandragupta II's father.

D is incorrect: Kumaragupta succeeded Chandragupta II.

💡 Kalidasa is famed for works like 'Abhijnanasakuntalam' and 'Meghaduta'.
Q.No 23 HistoryHindi
किस अधिनियम ने भारत में ईस्ट इंडिया कंपनी के शासन का आधिकारिक अंत चिह्नित किया?
A
Regulating Act, 1773
B
Government of India Act, 1858
✓ Correct
C
Indian Councils Act, 1861
D
Charter Act, 1833

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Government of India Act, 1858 marked the official end of East India Company rule, transferring power to the British Crown.

A is incorrect: The Regulating Act, 1773 began parliamentary control but did not end Company rule.

C is incorrect: The 1861 Act dealt with legislative councils.

D is incorrect: The 1833 Charter Act centralised the Company's administration.

💡 After 1858 India was governed by the Crown through a Secretary of State for India.
Q.No 25 HistoryEnglish
मझगांव डॉक को भारत सरकार ने किस वर्ष में अपने अधीन ले लिया था?
A
1972
B
1968
C
1960
✓ Correct
D
1964

Solution

Correct answer: C. Mazagon Dock was taken over by the Government of India in 1960 (it became a public-sector shipbuilder).

A is incorrect: 1972 is incorrect.

B is incorrect: 1968 is incorrect.

D is incorrect: 1964 is incorrect.

💡 Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders builds warships and submarines for the Indian Navy.
Q.No 24 HistoryHindi
अलाउद्दीन खिलजी के प्रशासन के तहत खेती पर निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा कर लगाया गया था?
A
महज़र
B
फिरोज़
C
आमिल
D
खराज
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Under Alauddin Khilji, 'Kharaj' (a land tax, often up to half the produce) was imposed on cultivation.

A is incorrect: 'Mahzar' was a legal document/declaration.

B is incorrect: 'Firoz' is not a tax.

C is incorrect: 'Amil' refers to a revenue official, not a tax.

💡 Alauddin Khilji introduced strict market and revenue controls to maintain a large army.
Q.No 36 HistoryHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
दिल्ली में 'लाल कोट' शहर का निर्माण किसने किया था?
A
पृथ्वी राज चौहान
B
अलाउद्दीन खिलजी
C
हुमायूं
D
अनंग पाल तोमर
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The city of 'Lal Kot' in Delhi was built by Anangpal Tomar (of the Tomar dynasty).

A is incorrect: Prithviraj Chauhan later extended it (Qila Rai Pithora) but did not found Lal Kot.

B is incorrect: Alauddin Khilji built Siri, a later city.

C is incorrect: Humayun built Dinpanah.

💡 Lal Kot is regarded as the first of Delhi's historic cities.
Q.No 21 HistoryHindi
छठी शताब्दी ईसा पूर्व में बौद्ध धर्म और जैन धर्म का उदय निम्नलिखित में से किससे जुड़ा हो सकता है?
A
राजशाही का उदय
B
वैदिक अनुष्ठानों की आलोचना
✓ Correct
C
कृषि का पतन
D
फ़ारसी प्रभाव

Solution

Correct answer: B. The rise of Buddhism and Jainism in the 6th century BCE is linked to a reaction against and criticism of complex Vedic rituals and the dominance of priests.

A is incorrect: The rise of monarchy is not the main cause of these religious movements.

C is incorrect: There was no decline of agriculture causing them.

D is incorrect: Persian influence was not the cause.

💡 Both religions rejected ritual sacrifices and emphasised non-violence and ethics.
Q.No 32 HistoryHindi
जम्मू और कश्मीर में शालीमार बाग का निर्माण द्वारा किया गया था।
A
जहाँगीर
✓ Correct
B
अकबर
C
बाबर
D
हुमायूँ

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Shalimar Bagh in Jammu and Kashmir (Srinagar) was built by the Mughal emperor Jahangir.

B is incorrect: Akbar did not build it.

C is incorrect: Babur did not build it.

D is incorrect: Humayun did not build it.

💡 Jahangir built the Shalimar Bagh for his wife Nur Jahan; it is a famous Mughal garden.
Q.No 24 HistoryHindi
मनसबदारी व्यवस्था में, मनसबदार द्वारा रखे जाने वाले घुड़सवार सैनिकों को किस शब्द से संदर्भित किया जाता था?
A
जात
B
सवार
✓ Correct
C
ज़मींदार
D
जागीर

Solution

Correct answer: B. In the Mansabdari system, the cavalry/horsemen a mansabdar had to maintain were indicated by the 'sawar' rank.

A is incorrect: 'Zat' indicated the mansabdar's personal rank/status.

C is incorrect: 'Zamindar' refers to a landholder.

D is incorrect: 'Jagir' was a land-revenue assignment.

💡 A mansabdar's rank had two numbers: 'zat' (status) and 'sawar' (cavalry to maintain).
Q.No 36 HistoryHindi
विट्ठलस्वामी मंदिर में स्थित है।
A
पुरी
B
हम्पी
✓ Correct
C
दिसपुर
D
ग्वालियर

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Vittala Swamy (Vitthala) Temple is located at Hampi, the former capital of the Vijayanagara Empire.

A is incorrect: It is not at Puri.

C is incorrect: It is not at Dispur.

D is incorrect: It is not at Gwalior.

💡 The Vittala Temple at Hampi is famous for its stone chariot and musical pillars.
Q.No 38 HistoryHindi
'लोकनायक' के नाम से लोकप्रिय कौन हैं जो "संपूर्ण क्रांति" की अवधारणा के लेखक कौन थे?
A
मोरारजी देसाई
B
राजेंद्र प्रसाद
C
लाल बहादुर शास्त्री
D
जयप्रकाश नारायण
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Jayaprakash Narayan, popularly known as 'Loknayak', gave the concept of 'Total Revolution' (Sampoorna Kranti).

A is incorrect: Morarji Desai did not give this concept.

B is incorrect: Rajendra Prasad was India's first President.

C is incorrect: Lal Bahadur Shastri gave 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan'.

💡 JP led the movement against the Emergency in the mid-1970s.
Q.No 21 HistoryHindi
झारखंड के किस आदिवासी राष्ट्रवादी को ब्रिटिश शोषण से लड़ने के लिए जाना जाता है और जिन्हें "धरती बाबा" के रूप में जाना जाता है, उन्होंने मुंडा विद्रोह का नेतृत्व किया और अब भारतीय संसद संग्रहालय में उनका स्मरण किया जाता है?
A
बिरसा मुंडा
✓ Correct
B
अल्लूरी सीताराम राजू
C
कोमरम भीम
D
पीर अली खान

Solution

Correct answer: A. Birsa Munda, the Jharkhand tribal nationalist known as 'Dharti Baba', led the Munda rebellion (Ulgulan) against British exploitation and is commemorated in the Indian Parliament Museum.

B is incorrect: Alluri Sitarama Raju led a revolt in Andhra Pradesh.

C is incorrect: Komaram Bheem was a Gond leader from Telangana.

D is incorrect: Pir Ali Khan was a 1857 rebel from Bihar.

💡 Birsa Munda's birth anniversary (15 November) is celebrated as Janjatiya Gaurav Divas.
Q.No 30 HistoryHindi
सिंधु घाटी सभ्यता के साक्ष्य वहां के लोगों के बारे में क्या बताते हैं?
A
वे व्यापक रूप से खानाबदोश थे
B
वे उत्कृष्ट इंजीनियर थे
✓ Correct
C
उनमें शहरी नियोजन कौशल का अभाव था
D
वे केवल वर्षा जल पर निर्भर थे

Solution

Correct answer: B. The evidence from the Indus Valley Civilization shows that its people were excellent engineers (with planned cities, drainage and the Great Bath).

A is incorrect: They were settled urban dwellers, not largely nomadic.

C is incorrect: They had advanced, not absent, urban-planning skills.

D is incorrect: They built wells and reservoirs, not relying only on rain.

💡 Harappan cities had grid layouts, standardised bricks and sophisticated drainage systems.
Q.No 39 HistoryHindi
|9वीं सदी के किस दार्शनिक और सुधारक ने सामाजिक-सांस्कृतिक संगठन 'आर्य समाज' की स्थापना की थी?
A
स्वामी विवेकानंद
B
दयानंद सरस्वती
✓ Correct
C
राजा राम मोहन राय
D
ईश्वर चंद्र विद्यासागर

Solution

Correct answer: B. The 19th-century philosopher and reformer Dayananda Saraswati founded the Arya Samaj (in 1875).

A is incorrect: Swami Vivekananda founded the Ramakrishna Mission.

C is incorrect: Raja Ram Mohan Roy founded the Brahmo Samaj.

D is incorrect: Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar was a reformer but did not found the Arya Samaj.

💡 The Arya Samaj gave the call to 'go back to the Vedas' and opposed social evils.
Q.No 33 HistoryEnglish
डेक्नन एयरवेज का राष्ट्रीयकरण किस वर्ष में किया गया था?
A
1949
B
1951
C
1955
D
1953
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Deccan Airways (along with other private airlines) was nationalised in 1953 under the Air Corporations Act.

A is incorrect: 1949 is incorrect.

B is incorrect: 1951 is incorrect.

C is incorrect: 1955 is incorrect.

💡 The 1953 nationalisation created Indian Airlines (domestic) and Air India (international).
Q.No 36 HistoryHindi
चन्द्रगुप्त प्रथम से विवाहित लिच्छवि राजकुमारी का नाम क्या था?
A
कदम्बदेवी
B
नयनिका
C
कुमारदेवी
✓ Correct
D
प्रभावती

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Lichchhavi princess married to Chandragupta I was named Kumaradevi.

A is incorrect: Kadambadevi is not the correct name.

B is incorrect: Nayanika was a Satavahana queen.

D is incorrect: Prabhavati was a Gupta princess married to a Vakataka king.

💡 The Gupta-Lichchhavi alliance strengthened Chandragupta I's position; coins commemorate this marriage.
Q.No 38 HistoryHindi
स्वाभिमान आंदोलन किसने शुरू किया था?
A
ई.वी. रामास्वामी नायकर
✓ Correct
B
घसीदास
C
श्री नारायण गुरु
D
ज्योतिराव फुले

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Self-Respect Movement (Swabhiman/Self-Respect) was started by E.V. Ramaswamy Naicker (Periyar).

B is incorrect: Ghasidas founded the Satnami movement.

C is incorrect: Sri Narayana Guru led reform in Kerala.

D is incorrect: Jyotirao Phule led the Satyashodhak Samaj in Maharashtra.

💡 Periyar's movement opposed caste discrimination and Brahminical dominance in Tamil Nadu.
Q.No 30 HistoryEnglish
गुरु गोबिंद सिंह ने खालसा पंथ की स्थापना किस वर्ष में की थी?
A
1606
B
1648
C
1676
D
1699
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Guru Gobind Singh founded the Khalsa Panth in 1699 (on Vaisakhi).

A is incorrect: 1606 is when Guru Arjan Dev was martyred.

B is incorrect: 1648 is incorrect.

C is incorrect: 1676 is incorrect.

💡 The Khalsa was founded at Anandpur Sahib with the initiation of the Panj Pyare.
Q.No 38 HistoryEnglish
निम्नलिखित में से किस दिन को मुस्लिम लीग द्वारा “प्रत्यक्ष कार्रवाई दिवस” मनाने का आह्वान किया था?
A
8 August 1942
B
9 December 1945
C
16 August 1946
✓ Correct
D
23 December 1946

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Muslim League called for 'Direct Action Day' on 16 August 1946 to press its demand for Pakistan.

A is incorrect: 8 August 1942 was the Quit India resolution.

B is incorrect: 9 December 1945 is incorrect.

D is incorrect: 23 December 1946 is incorrect.

💡 Direct Action Day led to large-scale communal riots, especially in Calcutta.
Q.No 26 HistoryHindi
निर्वासन के बाद मुगल सिंहासन पुनः प्राप्त करने में हुमायूं की सहायता किसने की?
A
गुजरात के बहादुर शाह ने
B
शेरशाह सूरी ने
C
फ़ारसी शासक शाह तहमास्प ने
✓ Correct
D
राणा सांगा ने

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Persian ruler Shah Tahmasp helped Humayun regain the Mughal throne after his exile.

A is incorrect: Bahadur Shah of Gujarat was an adversary.

B is incorrect: Sher Shah Suri had driven Humayun out.

D is incorrect: Rana Sanga was an earlier Rajput opponent of Babur.

💡 With Persian support, Humayun recovered Delhi in 1555, a year before his death.
Q.No 34 HistoryHindi
कनिष्क इतिहास में के महान संरक्षक के रूप में प्रसिद्ध है।
A
जैन धर्म
B
बुद्ध धर्म
✓ Correct
C
ईसाई धर्म
D
पारसी धर्म

Solution

Correct answer: B. Kanishka, the Kushan emperor, is famous in history as a great patron of Buddhism.

A is incorrect: He is not chiefly known as a patron of Jainism.

C is incorrect: He did not patronise Christianity.

D is incorrect: He is not associated with Zoroastrianism patronage.

💡 Kanishka convened the Fourth Buddhist Council and promoted Mahayana Buddhism.
Q.No 37 HistoryEnglish
निम्नलिखित में से किस वर्ष ब्रिटिश अधिकारियों ने विधवा विवाह के पक्ष में कानून पारित कर दिया था?
A
1833
B
1856
✓ Correct
C
1861
D
1885

Solution

Correct answer: B. The British passed the Hindu Widows' Remarriage Act in 1856, legalising the remarriage of Hindu widows.

A is incorrect: 1833 is incorrect.

C is incorrect: 1861 is incorrect.

D is incorrect: 1885 is the year the Congress was founded.

💡 Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar campaigned strongly for widow remarriage.
Q.No 23 HistoryEnglish
Which type of cultivation did the Gonds practice primarily?
A
Terrace cultivation
B
Permanent farming
C
Shifting cultivation
✓ Correct
D
Irrigation-based farming

Solution

Correct answer: C. The Gonds primarily practised shifting (slash-and-burn) cultivation.

A is incorrect: Terrace cultivation is practised in hilly north-east/Himalayan areas.

B is incorrect: They did not chiefly do permanent settled farming.

D is incorrect: They did not primarily use irrigation-based farming.

💡 Shifting cultivation involves clearing and burning forest patches for temporary farming.
Q.No 28 HistoryHindi
अशोक के धम्म का मुख्य उद्देश्य क्या था?
A
बौद्ध धर्म को राज्य धर्म के रूप में फैलाना
B
प्रशासनिक केंद्रीकरण को बढ़ावा देना
C
नैतिक जीवन और सामाजिक सद्धाव को प्रोत्साहित करना
✓ Correct
D
विदेशी शासकों के साथ गठबंधन स्थापित करना

Solution

Correct answer: C. The main aim of Ashoka's 'Dhamma' was to encourage moral living and social harmony among his subjects.

A is incorrect: It was not to impose Buddhism as a state religion.

B is incorrect: It was not about administrative centralisation.

D is incorrect: It was not about foreign alliances.

💡 Ashoka spread his Dhamma through rock and pillar edicts across his empire.
Q.No 31 HistoryHindi
किस यात्री के विवरण में मुहम्मद बिन तुगलक और उसके शासनकाल का साफ़-साफ़ वर्णन मिलता है?
A
इब्न बतूता
✓ Correct
B
मार्कों पोलो
C
अल-बिरूनी
D
डोमिंगो पेस

Solution

Correct answer: A. The traveller Ibn Battuta gave a clear account of Muhammad bin Tughlaq and his reign.

B is incorrect: Marco Polo travelled mainly to China and southern India earlier.

C is incorrect: Al-Biruni came with Mahmud of Ghazni, before the Tughlaqs.

D is incorrect: Domingo Paes described Vijayanagara, not the Tughlaqs.

💡 Ibn Battuta, a Moroccan traveller, served as a qazi (judge) in Delhi under Muhammad bin Tughlaq.
Q.No 35 HistoryEnglish
Rudrasena || was a ruler belonging to which dynasty?
A
Nanda dynasty
B
Maurya dynasty
C
Vakataka dynasty
✓ Correct
D
Gurjar-Pratihar dynasty

Solution

Correct answer: C. Rudrasena II was a ruler of the Vakataka dynasty (of the Deccan).

A is incorrect: He was not of the Nanda dynasty.

B is incorrect: He was not of the Maurya dynasty.

D is incorrect: He was not of the Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty.

💡 The Vakataka king Rudrasena II married Prabhavatigupta, daughter of Chandragupta II.
Q.No 37 HistoryEnglish
Who became the first Indian Governor-General of independent India?
A
C. Rajagopalachari
✓ Correct
B
Vallabh Bhai Patel
C
Rajendra Prasad
D
Jawaharlal Nehru

Solution

Correct answer: A. C. Rajagopalachari became the first Indian (and last overall) Governor-General of independent India.

B is incorrect: Vallabhbhai Patel was the first Home Minister/Deputy PM.

C is incorrect: Rajendra Prasad became the first President.

D is incorrect: Jawaharlal Nehru was the first Prime Minister.

💡 Lord Mountbatten was the first Governor-General after independence; Rajaji was the first Indian one.
Q.No 33 HistoryHindi
चन्द्रगुप्त प्रथम के बाद गुप्त साम्राज्य का शासक कौन बना था?
A
कुमारगुप्त द्वितीय
B
बुधगुप्त
C
समुद्रगुप्त
✓ Correct
D
चंद्रगुप्त द्वितीय

Solution

Correct answer: C. Samudragupta became the Gupta ruler after Chandragupta I.

A is incorrect: Kumaragupta II came much later.

B is incorrect: Budhagupta was a later ruler.

D is incorrect: Chandragupta II succeeded Samudragupta, not the other way round.

💡 Samudragupta is called the 'Napoleon of India' for his many military conquests.
Q.No 37 HistoryHindi
निम्नलिखित में से किस अधिनियम ने प्रांतीय स्वायत्तता का प्रावधान किया था?
A
Government of India Act, 1935
✓ Correct
B
Government of India Act, 1919
C
Government of India Act, 1909
D
Government of India Act, 1861

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Government of India Act, 1935 provided for provincial autonomy, ending dyarchy in the provinces.

B is incorrect: The 1919 Act introduced dyarchy in the provinces.

C is incorrect: 1909 is the Morley-Minto reforms.

D is incorrect: 1861 dealt with legislative councils.

💡 Under the 1935 Act, provincial elections were held in 1937.
Q.No 27 HistoryHindi
अशोक के जीवन में कौन-सी घटना एक निर्णायक मोड़ मानी जाती है, जिसने उन्हें धम्म अपनाने के लिए प्रेरित किया?
A
उनका राज्याभिषेक समारोह
B
कलिंग युद्ध
✓ Correct
C
श्रीलंका के साथ संधि
D
तक्षशिला का विद्रोह

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Kalinga War is considered the turning point that led Ashoka to embrace Dhamma after witnessing its bloodshed.

A is incorrect: His coronation was not the turning point.

C is incorrect: A treaty with Sri Lanka was not the cause.

D is incorrect: The Taxila revolt was not the turning point.

💡 After the Kalinga War, Ashoka renounced violent conquest in favour of 'conquest by Dhamma'.
Q.No 29 HistoryHindi
चोल प्रशासन में 'उर' का मुख्य काम क्या था?
A
सैन्य भर्ती
B
मंदिर निर्माण
C
ग्राम स्तरीय शासन
✓ Correct
D
विदेशी व्यापारियों से कर संग्रह

Solution

Correct answer: C. In Chola administration, the 'Ur' was the assembly responsible for village-level governance (in ordinary villages).

A is incorrect: It was not for military recruitment.

B is incorrect: It was not for temple construction.

D is incorrect: It was not for collecting taxes from foreign traders.

💡 The Cholas had a sophisticated local self-government system, including the Ur, Sabha and Nagaram.
Q.No 34 HistoryHindi
मगध में शिशुनाग वंश के बाद कौन सा राजवंश आया था?
A
गुप्त वंश
B
नंद वंश
✓ Correct
C
मौर्य वंश
D
हर्यक राजवंश

Solution

Correct answer: B. In Magadha, the Nanda dynasty came after the Shishunaga dynasty.

A is incorrect: The Gupta dynasty came much later.

C is incorrect: The Maurya dynasty came after the Nandas.

D is incorrect: The Haryanka dynasty came before the Shishunagas.

💡 The Nanda dynasty was overthrown by Chandragupta Maurya (with Chanakya's help).
Q.No 37 HistoryHindi
यंग बंगाल मूवमेंट किसने शुरू किया था?
A
हेनरी लुई विवियन डेरोज़ियो
✓ Correct
B
केशब चंद्र सेन
C
राजा राममोहन राय
D
एलेन ओक्टेवियन ह्यूम

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Young Bengal Movement was started by Henry Louis Vivian Derozio.

B is incorrect: Keshab Chandra Sen was a Brahmo Samaj leader.

C is incorrect: Raja Ram Mohan Roy founded the Brahmo Samaj.

D is incorrect: A.O. Hume founded the Indian National Congress.

💡 Derozio inspired his students at Hindu College to question tradition and embrace rationalism.
Q.No 26 HistoryHindi
आगरा किले में कौन-सी संरचना अपने सफेद संगमरमर के मेहराबों के लिए जानी जाती है और इसका उपयोग शाहजहाँ ने ताजमहल देखने के लिए किया था?
A
मुसम्मन बुर्ज
✓ Correct
B
मोती मस्जिद
C
नगीना मस्जिद
D
दीवान-ए-खास

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Musamman Burj in the Agra Fort, with its white-marble arches, is where Shah Jahan was imprisoned and reportedly gazed at the Taj Mahal.

B is incorrect: The Moti Masjid is a pearl mosque, not this tower.

C is incorrect: The Nagina Masjid was the ladies' mosque.

D is incorrect: The Diwan-i-Khas was the hall of private audience.

💡 Shah Jahan spent his last years confined in the Agra Fort by his son Aurangzeb.
Q.No 35 HistoryHindi
चन्द्रगुप्त द्वितीय के दरबार में नौ दिग्गजों (नवरत्नों) में से सबसे उल्लेखनीय किसे माना जाता था?
A
तुलसीदास
B
कालिदास
✓ Correct
C
सूरदास
D
बाणभट्ट

Solution

Correct answer: B. Among the 'Navaratnas' (nine gems) of Chandragupta II's court, the poet Kalidasa is considered the most notable.

A is incorrect: Tulsidas lived much later (16th century).

C is incorrect: Surdas lived later.

D is incorrect: Banabhatta was in Harshavardhana's court.

💡 Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya) is associated with the legendary nine gems of his court.
Q.No 36 HistoryHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा अधिनियम उन समाचार पत्रों को चुप कराने के प्रयास में लागू किया गया जो सरकार की आलोचना करते थे?
A
Vernacular Press Act, 1878
✓ Correct
B
Vernacular Press Act, 1858
C
Charter Act, 1833
D
Charter Act, 1813

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Vernacular Press Act, 1878 was enacted to silence Indian-language newspapers that criticised the government.

B is incorrect: There was no Vernacular Press Act in 1858.

C is incorrect: The 1833 Charter Act dealt with administration.

D is incorrect: The 1813 Charter Act dealt with the Company's monopoly.

💡 The Act was passed under Viceroy Lord Lytton and later repealed by Lord Ripon.
Q.No 35 HistoryHindi
सल्तनत के तहत गन्ने जैसी नई फसलों और बेहतर सिंचाई की शुरूआत के परिणामस्वरूप कया हुआ?
A
गुज़ारा करने वाली खेती में गिरावट
B
खेती में विविधता और ज़्यादा सरप्लस
✓ Correct
C
आयात पर ज़्यादा निर्भरता
D
पारंपरिक फसलों को छोड़ देना

Solution

Correct answer: B. Under the Delhi Sultanate, introducing new crops like sugarcane and better irrigation led to diversification of farming and greater agricultural surplus.

A is incorrect: It did not cause a decline in subsistence farming.

C is incorrect: It did not increase dependence on imports.

D is incorrect: It did not lead to abandoning traditional crops.

💡 Sultanate rulers like Firoz Shah Tughlaq built canals to expand irrigation.
Q.No 38 HistoryHindi
खान अब्दुल गफ्फार खान को निम्नलिखित में से अन्य किस नाम से भी जाना जाता था?
A
बाबा-ए-कौम
B
राजा जी
C
सरदार
D
बादशाह खान
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan was also known as 'Badshah Khan' (and 'Frontier Gandhi').

A is incorrect: 'Baba-e-Qaum' is a title for Jinnah in Pakistan.

B is incorrect: 'Rajaji' refers to C. Rajagopalachari.

C is incorrect: 'Sardar' refers to Vallabhbhai Patel.

💡 Ghaffar Khan founded the non-violent Khudai Khidmatgar movement.
